name: Sierra de Crevillente Loop
name_original: Sierra la Vella de Crevillent — Circular por la Subida Catí
description: This difficult 20.6-mile mountain biking loop in Sierra de Crevillent offers demanding terrain and epic views from the La Vella Antennas.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Crevillent are set within the Sierra de Crevillent mountain range, offering a diverse landscape for outdoor enthusiasts. This region features rugged peaks, valleys, and Mediterranean scrubland, providing a varied terrain for mountain biking. Pine forests and fascinating rock formations characterize the area, with the highest peak, Sant Gaietà, reaching 835 meters. The network of trails caters to a wide range of skill levels, from challenging ascents to more moderate paths. Best mountain bike trails around Crevillent The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Crevillente Loop, a 20.6 miles (33.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the heart of the Sierra de Crevillent. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is The Pontets Aqueduct – La Vella Antennas loop from Crevillent, a difficult 17.4 miles (27.9 km) path. This route offers demanding terrain and panoramic views from the La Vella Antennas. Local mountain bikers also love the The Pontets Aqueduct – Ermita de la Purísima loop from Aparcamiento Tetería Carmen del Campillo, a 24.9 miles (40.1 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Crevillent, often completed in about 3 hours 13 minutes. Mountain biking around Crevillent is defined by rugged peaks, pine forests, and historic aqueducts. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Crevillent are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Crevillent's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Crevillent?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails available around Crevillent, offering a wide range of experiences. These routes are set within the diverse landscape of the Sierra de Crevillent, featuring rugged peaks, valleys, and Mediterranean scrubland.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Crevillent?
A: Crevillent offers a good mix of difficulty levels. You'll find 3 easy trails, 19 moderate trails, and 19 difficult trails. This variety ensures options for different skill levels, from those seeking a relaxed ride to experienced mountain bikers looking for a challenge.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Crevillent?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Crevillent are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927542778/sierra-de-crevillente-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Crevillente Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult circular route covering 33.2 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Crevillent?
A: The terrain in Crevillent is varied, primarily within the Sierra de Crevillent mountain range. You can expect rugged peaks, lush valleys, Mediterranean scrubland, and pine forests. Some routes feature fascinating rock formations and challenging technical sections, while others offer more moderate paths.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural features along the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra de Crevillent offers panoramic views, especially from higher points like Sant Gaietà (835 meters) and the La Vella Antennas. You can also find natural features such as the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31407194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Pontets Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt;, which serves as a starting point for several routes. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Anguilla Observatory&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Niño Bird Observatory&lt;/a&gt; offer great spots for views and wildlife spotting near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6421217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Crevillente&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in Crevillent suitable for e-bikes?
A: Yes, many trails in the Sierra de Crevillent are well-suited for e-bikes. The varied terrain, including challenging ascents, can be more easily enjoyed with the assistance of an e-bike, making more routes accessible to a wider range of riders.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Crevillent?
A: The mountain bike routes in Crevillent are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Many users praise the diverse terrain, challenging climbs, and the rewarding panoramic views from points like the La Vella Antennas.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by historical landmarks?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical elements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27634283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Pontets Aqueduct – Ermita de la Purísima loop&lt;/a&gt; starts near the historic Els Pontets Aqueduct, an Islamic-era structure dating back to the 13th century, adding a cultural dimension to your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Crevillent?
A: Crevillent generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. The Mediterranean climate means mild winters, making it a viable option even in cooler months. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures, while summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or canyons to see near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region around Crevillent features some impressive natural attractions. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4852429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chicamo River Canyon&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6136157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rambla Salada&lt;/a&gt; gorge. There's also the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4444388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Final Waterfall of the Via Ferrata&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2440117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elche Reservoir and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored as part of a longer day out.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33921848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vella Antennas – Catí Ascent loop from Crevillent&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 46.9 km route with significant elevation gain, offering a substantial challenge and rewarding views.

Q: Can I find trails that lead to high points with extensive views?
A: Definitely. Many trails in the Sierra de Crevillent ascend to high points offering expansive vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31711033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico La Vella – La Vella Antennas loop from Crevillent&lt;/a&gt;, for example, takes you to Pico La Vella, known for its panoramic views over the surrounding landscape.
tips: Viewpoint par excellence in the Sierra de Crevillent. Look for the best view avoiding the antennas, you won't regret it!
Very picturesque stretch of road with beautiful views in all directions, with beautiful views of the Mediterranean Sea.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Sierra de Crevillente Loop?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike route is quite challenging and covers a distance of approximately 33 km with nearly 1,000 meters of elevation gain. On average, riders can expect to spend around 3 hours and 27 minutes on the trail, but this can vary significantly based on skill level and stops.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Crevillente Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, the Sierra de Crevillente Loop is rated as difficult. The terrain includes 'chunky' sections, 'rock gardens,' narrow 'counter-banked trails with rock sections,' and 'loose stone.' Some segments are explicitly unsuitable for riders of medium-low skill levels, making it more appropriate for experienced mountain bikers.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Sierra de Crevillente Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations for this exact loop are not detailed, the Sierra de Crevillente area generally offers various access points. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot's starting point for the most convenient parking near the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit the Sierra de Crevillente for mountain biking is typically from September to December. Southern Spain enjoys a pleasant climate during these months, making it ideal for outdoor activities and avoiding the intense summer heat.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Sierra de Crevillente Loop or the Sierra de Crevillent park?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access the Sierra de Crevillente Loop or the general Sierra de Crevillent park area for mountain biking.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Crevillente Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify whether dogs are allowed on this particular loop. When riding in natural areas, it's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are well-behaved around other trail users and wildlife.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Expect a varied and demanding terrain. The route features 'chunky' sections, 'rock gardens,' and narrow, 'counter-banked trails with rock sections.' You'll also encounter paths with 'loose stone' that can be very fast, alongside some asphalted sections. The substantial elevation changes contribute to the challenging nature of the ride.

faqs.question: What notable features or viewpoints will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass by significant highlights such as the La Vella Antennas, which offer picturesque views of the mountainous landscape. You'll also experience the challenging Catí Ascent, a notable climb in the area. The trail often provides 'epic views' of the rugged Sierra de Crevillente.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the Sierra de Crevillent area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route integrates sections of several other trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Sierra de Crevillente (37% of the route), Sierra de Crevillent (30%), and El Camí IT5TR3 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana (22%). Other smaller segments include Els Anouers, camí de l'antena, Subida Catí, Trialera del tío Ginés, Paso de las Yeguas, Trialera Parque de Montaña, La Culebra, Interminable, Subida a la Vella, and Cresteo del pantano.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or water sources along the route?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify the presence of cafes or dedicated water sources directly along this particular loop. It's always recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your ride, especially on a difficult and lengthy route like this.

faqs.question: What's a good strategy for tackling the challenging sections of this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and descriptions of 'chunky' sections and 'rock gardens,' a good strategy involves being prepared for technical riding. Consider a bike with good suspension and be ready to navigate loose stones and narrow, counter-banked trails. Pacing yourself on the significant climbs, like the Catí Ascent, will also be key to conserving energy.
